SOS: Symptoms or Stress
✦ Teen-friendly health learning

SOS: Symptoms or Stress

Because Googling symptoms shouldn’t feel terrifying.

A calm, educational app that helps teens think through symptoms, learn basic clinical reasoning, and understand when stress may be part of the picture.

Symptoms
2
Stress
8/10
Cases
3
🫀

Symptom Checker

Select symptoms and answer a few calm check-in questions.
Stress level 8/10
How long has this been going on?
Assessment

Likely stress-influenced

Educational, not diagnostic
Stress influence estimate 63%
Next step:
🧠

Learn Mode

Understand the why behind symptoms.
Stress can feel physical
Stress does not just stay in your mind. It can show up as headaches, nausea, fatigue, chest tightness, or trouble sleeping.
Patterns matter
Doctors look for timing, severity, triggers, and what other symptoms happen together. Your app can teach that kind of thinking.
Red flags matter too
Some symptoms need quicker attention, especially if they are severe, sudden, or keep getting worse.
🌬️

Calm Check

Support the nervous system too.
Quick reset
Inhale for 4, hold for 4, exhale for 6. Repeat three times and check whether your symptoms shift at all.
Gentle reminders
  • Hydrate and eat something nourishing.
  • Notice whether stress seems to trigger symptoms.
  • Ask for help if anything feels intense or unusual.
This app is for learning and reflection. It does not replace a doctor, urgent care, or emergency support.

Case Challenges

Practice clinical reasoning in a way that still feels approachable.
Reasoning

Why this project stands out

Built for teens, grounded in health education, and designed with empathy.
Combines symptom awareness, stress education, and calm design instead of creating more anxiety.
Shows your interest in clinical reasoning, pathology, teen health access, and thoughtful product design.
Easy to expand later with HOSA practice, real educational modules, or physician-reviewed content.
Built as a student project concept for health education and clinical reasoning practice.
